Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 035. The Case of Nabu-mušallim (ABL 0730)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[a-na LUGAL be-lí]-ia ARAD-ka m30—PAB-MEŠ—SU

(1) [To the king, my] lord: your servant Sin-ahhe-riba. [Good health] to the king, my lord! Assyria [is well], the temples are well, all the king's forts [are well]. The king, my lord, can be glad indeed.

o 22

[lu šul-mu a]-na LUGAL EN-ia

o 33

[šul-mu a-na KUR]—aš-šur.KI šul-mu a-na É.KUR-MEŠ

o 44

[šul-mu a-na URU].bi-rat ša LUGAL gab-bu

o 55

[lib-bu ša LUGAL EN]-ia a—dan-niš lu DÙG.GA

o 66

[ša LUGAL be-lí iš-pur-an]-⸢ni* ma⸣-a mEN—KAR-ir

(6) [As to what the king, my lord, wrote to m]e: "Bel-eṭir [......

o 77

[x x x x x x x x]+⸢x⸣-ir-šú-nu

rest broken away

(Break)

Reverse
rr beginning broken away

r 1'1'

[x x x x x] ⸢ma*⸣-a ni-⸢x⸣+[x x x x]+⸢x⸣+[x]

r 2'2'

[x x x x] ⸢ma*⸣-a mdAG—KAR-ir—ZI-MEŠ

(r 2) "......] Nabû-eṭir-napšati (and) his associate [...], these are the ones [......]; their men [are] still [......]. When I told this thing [to Nabû-m]ušallim, he disappeared [......]."

r 3'3'

[x x x x x] i-se-e-šú ma-a an-nu-te šú-nu

r 4'4'

[x x x x] SAG ma-a ERIM-MEŠ-šu-nu aḫ—⸢ḫur⸣

r 5'5'

[x x x x]+⸢x⸣-ia-ak ma-a ki a-bu-tú an-ni-⸢tú⸣

r 6'6'

[a-na mdPA]—⸢mu⸣-šal-lim a-qa-bu-ú-ni

r 7'7'

[x x x x] ⸢ma⸣ iḫ-ti-liq šu-[tú]

(r 7) [...] I asked my [...] whether [he] had said it and whether Nabû-mušallim [had told it to him].

r 8'8'

[a-na x]+⸢x⸣-ia a-sa-al nu-uk qa-⸢bi⸣-i

Right
r.e. 9r.e. 9

[x x x]-⸢bi*⸣ nu-uk m*dPA*—⸢mu*⸣-[šal]-lim

r.e. 1010

[iq-ṭí-ba-ka]-a ma-a la-a-⸢šú*⸣ [la] ⸢iq⸣-bi-a

(r 10) He answered: "He certainly did not tell it to me. [Nabû]-mušallim [has ...ed] from Calah and is searching [...]."

r.e. 1111

[ma-a mdPA]—⸢mu*-šal*⸣-lim TAv URU.kal-ḫa

r.e. 1212

[x x x x x]+⸢x⸣ ú-ba-ia

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334519/.
